spec_match.rationale: Pairs `performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory()` with crashReporter.set so the OOM crash arrives with last-good metrics — strong real-world spec application.

interactive.rationale: Three live gauges (heap / DOM / observers), grow/shrink stress buttons, snapshot button, crash simulator.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises live gauges with a 'grow allocations' button; page delivers all three.

edge_cases.rationale: Uses `measureUserAgentSpecificMemory` but doesn't show what to do when it's unavailable (Safari, older Chrome), nor how to handle the async promise inside a synchronous crash handler.

references.rationale: References section needs verification — chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 required per invariant 3.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, .bar.warn/.danger use --accent-amber/--accent-rose correctly, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate measureMemory unavailability path
openQuestions.detail: `performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory` is gated to crossOriginIsolated and not present everywhere. The demo should detect and surface that, falling back to `performance.memory` (Chrome) or null with an honest message about reduced fidelity.

openQuestions.title: Show the timing race
openQuestions.detail: If the crash happens before snapshotMemory's promise resolves, the KV is stale. Add a control to deliberately race them — fire the crash immediately after starting a measurement — so authors see when last-good is stale-good.

openQuestions.title: Verify chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Confirm see-also includes chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536; add if absent (invariant 3).

summary: Genuinely useful OOM-debugging pattern wired up correctly. To strengthen, model the unavailable-API and timing-race cases that production code must handle.
